name: Ontario
contextBlurb: Ontario is positioning itself as Canada's leading AI and data center hub. Bill 40 (December 2025) lets the Energy Minister prioritize data center grid connections by economic benefit, replacing first-come-first-served. The province's Trustworthy AI Framework sets risk-based transparency rules for public-sector AI, and Bill 149 brought AI-in-hiring disclosure into effect in January 2026 — while the grid operator projects commercial data center demand to grow over 400% by 2050.

legislation.title: Protect Ontario by Securing Affordable Energy for Generations Act, 2025
legislation.summary: Enacted December 11, 2025, this law amends the Electricity Act and Ontario Energy Board Act to embed economic growth as a formal regulatory objective and grants the Minister of Energy and Mines new authority to approve and prioritize large data centre grid connections based on economic, strategic, and community benefit criteria. It ends the prior first-come, first-served connection regime for high-demand facilities and restricts foreign participation in Ontario's energy sector.

legislation.title: Working for Workers Four Act, 2024
legislation.summary: Received Royal Assent March 21, 2024, with AI-disclosure provisions taking effect January 1, 2026. Ontario employers with 25 or more employees must now disclose in all publicly advertised job postings whether artificial intelligence is used to screen, assess, or select applicants.

legislation.title: Ontario Artificial Intelligence, Talent and Innovation Strategy Act, 2025
legislation.summary: A private member's bill introduced November 2025 by NDP MPP Rob Cerjanec that would require the provincial government to design and maintain a comprehensive AI talent and innovation strategy with the goal of making Ontario a global AI leader within ten years. It would establish an AI Advisory Committee to publish annual progress reports and recommend legislative changes.

legislation.billCode: Working for Workers Four Act — AI disclosure
legislation.title: AI Disclosure in Job Postings (effective 1 Jan 2026)
legislation.summary: Effective 1 January 2026, Ontario employers with 25+ employees must disclose in publicly advertised job postings if they use AI to screen, assess or select applicants. The provision builds on the Working for Workers Four Act and aligns with the province's January 2026 joint IPC/OHRC principles for responsible AI use.

legislation.title: Microsoft Multi-Billion AI Infrastructure Expansion in Ontario
legislation.summary: Announced 7 April 2026 with Invest Ontario, Microsoft's expansion of its Azure Canada Central region includes two new York Region data centres as part of its $19 billion Canadian commitment. Projects will create ~1,000 construction and 250 permanent jobs, tied to a Community-First approach emphasising workforce training and digital sovereignty.

legislation.title: Ontario IPC + OHRC Responsible AI Principles (Jan 2026)
legislation.summary: In January 2026 Ontario's Information and Privacy Commissioner and Human Rights Commission jointly released six principles for responsible public-sector AI use, covering transparency, accountability, non-discrimination, privacy, explainability and human oversight. The principles guide implementation of Ontario's Enhancing Digital Security and Trust Act.
